title: Create feeds.yaml for documentation advisor scan configuration
perceive: Documentation advisor's context_prompt declares feeds.yaml as input source. File does not exist. Scan should declare its scope and data sources formally.
execute: - scan_scope: which directories and file types to scan (e.g., *.md files, WAI-Spoke docs, root docs)
- data_sources: explicit list of directories to include in scan (prompts/, spec/, WAI-Spoke/, etc.)
- output_preferences: e.g., impact threshold for creating lugs, tag classifications
- exclusions: paths/patterns to skip (e.g., .git/, node_modules/, samples/)
2. Structure should be compatible with how other advisors use feeds.yaml (check framework_development or expediter for pattern).
3. Verify it can be read by next advisory scan run without errors.
verify: feeds.yaml exists, contains scan_scope and data_sources sections, is valid YAML, can be read during next documentation advisor scan.
acceptance_criteria: File created with proper YAML structure, documented, no parse errors.
